qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Docs API accepts attacker-controlled output paths and writes files outside the intended target
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory is a code-injection flaw in @orval/core comment generation from unsanitized x-enumDescriptions handled by jsStringEscape, while this finding is a separate docs API path-handling issue that forwards attacker-controlled output.schemas/output.workspace into filesystem writes.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described root cause: unsanitized */ handling in jsStringEscape during x-enumDescriptions comment emission. The only final qualifying finding is a distinct docs API arbitrary file-write path.
evidence: The advisory JSON describes code injection via unsanitized x-enumDescriptions values that break out of JavaScript comment blocks because jsStringEscape fails to sanitize * and / characters.

The only final qualifying finding in .judge/input_manifest.json is docs_api_arbitrary_file_write, which reports attacker-controlled output.schemas/output.workspace reaching filesystem writes through the docs POST /api/generate flow.

The finding report, PoC report, and reproduction bundle discuss arbitrary file creation under attacker-chosen directories; they do not reference enum description comments, jsStringEscape, comment breakout, or generated-code injection.
